notes: Two-pose layout and overall orientation roughly mirror the photo and a few FAO cues (sparse stipple, ring suckers, eye marker) are present, but proportions are stretched, arms read as a noisy blob rather than individual appendages, and edge-tracing artifacts undermine the scientific-plate restraint.
specific_issues: body proportions horizontally stretched/flattened vs the photo
individual arms not delineated; arm cluster reads as a single mass with internal noise
stray horizontal line artifact extending past the silhouette in the top view
edge-tracing noise on mantle and fin outline rather than a clean continuous contour
sucker rings inconsistent and incomplete along the arms
eye lacks the almond shape and small filled pupil of FAO plates
long tentacular clubs from the photo not clearly resolved
fin/mantle junction shape on the top squid distorted compared to the photo
